_schema.purpose: A per-screen CAPTURE CONTRACT: how to deterministically reach and capture one legacy screen so the evidence is real (usable, not just 'rendered'). The driver agent writes one of these per screen; capture_screen.py reads it with --profile; the agent reuses the SAME profile to capture the React side, guaranteeing comparable captures.
rules: Prefer semantic readiness (waitFor + mustContain + waitForGone) over a big waitMs; waitMs is the LAST small settle, not the strategy.
Record the canonical route AND the misleading routes (e.g. a direct *.do that shows an error page).
workflow may be an inline list of steps (navigate/click/fill/select/wait) OR a path to a steps JSON.
Use the same profile (minus url) for the legacy and react captures.
recordHar:true records the REAL backend responses (legacy.har) for record-mode replay (no fake data).
errorSignatures[] = text that marks a wrong/error page; a hit QUARANTINES the capture to _rejected/ instead of promoting it. The app's login route is added automatically from project.loginAction.
dataMode (record|live|api) tells the agent how this view's React side gets real data; capture itself ignores it.
Values below are GENERIC EXAMPLES — substitute the app's context root, routes, field names (from project.json), and feature labels.

examples.notes: Static pre-auth page; minimal settle. Field names come from project.loginFields.

examples.authContext: authenticated via the REAL login-form submit; entity context <entity>. Reuse auth_state.json (--auth-state).
mustContain: <a label that proves the real screen loaded>
knownFailureModes: Opening the dispatcher route WITHOUT a live logged-in session shows the login/error route, not the screen.
A direct login route is a MISLEADING target (standalone page) — never use it as a verification route (auto-added to errorSignatures from project.loginAction).
Data widgets may hydrate several seconds after the first HTML; capturing too early yields missing backend data.
examples.notes: Reach by submitting the real login form first (or reuse auth_state.json), THEN this dispatcher route. Tune the settle to the app's slowest widget (see runtime-readiness-and-auth.md).

examples.canonicalRoute: loginAction.do (as a FORM POST, not a direct GET) -> dispatcher summary
examples.authContext: performs the real login, then lands on the authenticated summary
knownFailureModes: The app may try to recover auth via platform paths that don't exist on localhost — see runtime-readiness-and-auth.md.
examples.notes: Use this when you must prove the real login navigation rather than reusing a saved session.
